How to Use Kanban View in GoHighLevel — Manage Deals Better (For Indian Agencies)

You're running a digital agency in Bangalore or Mumbai. You have 15-20 active deals across different stages: discovery calls, proposals, negotiations, closing. Your team is spread across WhatsApp, email, spreadsheets, and maybe Zoho CRM. Every morning, you waste 30 minutes just figuring out which deals need attention today. By Friday, you've lost count of how many leads fell through the cracks.

This is the reality for most Indian digital agencies scaling from 5-10 people to 20+. Managing deals without visibility kills your conversion rate. You're not lacking talent or clients—you're drowning in disorganized information.

The Real Problem: Why Your Pipeline Isn't Working

When you're bootstrapping or running lean in a tier-2 city like Pune or Hyderabad, you can't afford dedicated sales ops people. So your account managers are juggling multiple CRMs, sending deal updates via WhatsApp messages, and hoping nothing slips. Result? Your ₹5 lakh opportunity gets delayed by a week because the proposal got lost in a Slack conversation.

Most Indian agencies we speak with are trapped between two extremes:

  • Spreadsheet chaos: Beautiful in theory, nightmare in execution. No real-time collaboration, no automation, no historical context.
  • Over-complicated CRM: Tools like Zoho CRM are powerful but require months of setup and constant admin work. Your team barely uses half the features.

You need something in between—a visual, drag-and-drop deal board that your team actually uses. Enter GoHighLevel's Kanban View.

What is Kanban View in GoHighLevel?

Kanban View is a visual sales pipeline management tool inside GoHighLevel's Opportunities module. Instead of scrolling through endless rows in a spreadsheet, you see your deals as cards organized into columns—one column per pipeline stage (Lead, Qualified, Proposal Sent, Negotiation, Won, Lost).

Think of it like a physical whiteboard with sticky notes—except it's digital, collaborative, and connected to WhatsApp automation.

Key benefits for Indian agencies:

  • See your entire ₹50 lakh pipeline in one glance
  • Drag-and-drop deals between stages (no clunky form fills)
  • Collapse stages to reduce noise and focus on what matters
  • Bulk actions to move multiple deals at once (e.g., send Razorpay payment links to 5 won deals simultaneously)
  • WhatsApp integration to notify clients when their deal moves to "Final Review"
  • Zero learning curve—your team gets it on day one

Real Use Case: How a Mumbai Agency Saved ₹10 Lakhs with Better Deal Visibility

Raj runs a 12-person digital marketing agency in Powai, Mumbai. His team manages roughly 25-30 active opportunities at any time, ranging from ₹2 lakhs to ₹25 lakhs per project. Before GoHighLevel, his conversion rate was 35%—industry average but not great.

His biggest problem? Deals getting stuck in "Proposal Sent" for weeks. No one knew if the client was waiting, delayed, or ready to sign. Raj was checking Zoho CRM sporadically and missing follow-up windows.

After implementing GoHighLevel's Kanban View:

  • Established a clear visual rule: "Deals in Proposal Sent stay there max 5 days, then auto-trigger a WhatsApp follow-up"
  • His team could now see deal status at a glance during morning standups
  • Used bulk actions to move 3 proposals to "Closed Won" in one click and instantly send Razorpay payment links via WhatsApp
  • Conversion rate jumped to 48% in 90 days
  • Recovered ~₹10 lakhs from deals that would have otherwise ghosted

This is possible because visibility drives action.

How to Set Up and Use Kanban View (Step-by-Step)

Step 1: Access Your Kanban Board

In GoHighLevel, go to Opportunities → Kanban View. You'll see your pipeline stages as columns. If you haven't created stages yet, set them up: Lead → Qualified → Proposal → Negotiation → Won/Lost.

Step 2: Drag and Drop Deals Between Stages

Click any deal card and drag it to the next stage. This is your primary workflow. A deal moves from "Lead" to "Qualified" when you've had the discovery call. From "Qualified" to "Proposal" when you've sent the proposal. It's that simple.

Step 3: Collapse Stages You Don't Need Today

If you're focused on closing deals, collapse the "Lead" and "Qualified" columns to reduce visual clutter. Kanban remembers your preference next time you log in.

Step 4: Use Bulk Actions for Speed

Select multiple deals (e.g., all won deals) → Click "Bulk Actions" → Choose "Send WhatsApp Message" or "Update Stage." This saves hours of manual work each week.

Step 5: Add Custom Fields Relevant to Your Business

Add fields like "Budget Approved by Finance?" or "Razorpay Link Sent?" directly to the Kanban cards. Your team sees everything at a glance.

WhatsApp Automation: The Game Changer for Indian Agencies

Here's where GoHighLevel becomes invaluable for India specifically. Unlike competitors like Zoho CRM, GoHighLevel has native WhatsApp integration. No fiddling with APIs. No relying on third-party tools like Twilio.

Simple automation flow:

  • Deal moves to "Proposal Sent" → Automatically send client a WhatsApp with the proposal PDF
  • Deal moves to "Negotiation" → Send WhatsApp reminder to your team: "Client negotiating. Follow up tomorrow."
  • Deal moves to "Won" → Send Razorpay UPI payment link via WhatsApp with invoice

No email delays. No dead links. Just instant, personal WhatsApp notifications that clients actually see.

Starter Plan: $97/month (~₹8,000/month) — Perfect for agencies with <50 deals/month

Factor in GST (18%), you're looking at ~₹29,200/month for the Agency Plan. ROI calculation for a ₹50 lakh quarterly pipeline:

  • If Kanban View + WhatsApp automation improves your conversion rate by just 5% (e.g., 40% → 42%), that's an additional ₹5 lakhs in annual revenue
  • At 40% margins, that's ₹2 lakhs net profit per year
  • Annual tool cost: ₹3.5 lakhs
  • Still 4x ROI in year one, plus the time savings for your team

Try GoHighLevel free for 30 days to run these numbers with your actual deals.

FAQs on Kanban View for Indian Agencies

Can I customize the pipeline stages to match my specific workflow?

Yes. You can create unlimited stages and rename them. Some agencies use: Lead → Warm Lead → Proposal → Closed. Others use: Inbound → Qualified → Negotiation → Contract Signed → Invoice Sent. Design it around your actual sales process, not the template.

Does Kanban View work on mobile for my team in different cities?

Partially. GoHighLevel has a mobile app, but Kanban is optimized for desktop. Your team can view and update deals from their phone, but drag-and-drop works best on laptop/tablet.

What if my team is already using Zoho CRM? Can I migrate?

Yes. GoHighLevel supports data imports from most CRMs. The migration process takes 2-4 hours for a typical agency with 100-200 historical deals. Support from GoHighLevel's India-friendly team is available via email.

Can I set up automatic reminders if a deal stays in one stage too long?

Yes. Use GoHighLevel's workflow automation: "If deal has been in Proposal Sent stage for 7 days, send team a WhatsApp reminder." This prevents deals from rotting.

Ready to Stop Losing Deals?

If you're managing a sales pipeline with spreadsheets or a bloated CRM, Kanban View is a game-changer. Your Mumbai, Bangalore, or Pune team will actually use it because it mirrors how humans naturally think about workflow stages.
